methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $2.4B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $4.0B.

data.narrative:
Insitro, a Series C biotech and health company founded by Daphne Koller, has raised $700M+ and carries a current post-money valuation of $2.4B. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 11 comparable companies across the biotech and health sector—3 public (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private firms at similar or adjacent stages, including Ultima Genomics, Eikon Therapeutics, and HistoSonics.

Two valuation methods triangulate to a defensible range. The stage-progression approach applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $4.1B. The peer-median method anchors to 9 same-sector comparables spanning $600M to $4.0B, with a median valuation of $1.7B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiple is inapplicable because private-company revenue is not disclosed. The public sector median P/S of 7.4x cannot be deployed without Insitro's top-line data.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $1.4B (low) to $4.7B (high), with a median of $2.9B. The current $2.4B valuation sits within this envelope, slightly below the median. Key uncertainties include revenue scale and growth trajectory—critical inputs for public-comp validation—and the timing and terms of the next financing round, which will materially influence realized post-money value. The wide peer range ($600M to $4.0B) reflects heterogeneity in stage and technology maturity within the cohort.
